core_move: Sweeten the character or content with shared references that make the audience recognize themselves.
Design visuals, text hierarchy, and action cues as one integrated system that controls where the eye goes and what the hand does next.
Structure the emotional path of an experience so the peak intensity and the remembered ending compound to create lasting trust and memory.
Organize the user experience or content piece along a deliberate narrative arc, giving it a clear beginning, a tension-building middle, and a resolving end.
Optimize the content and interface for the user's real emotional state, device,  and physical moment of use, rather than for aesthetic purity or the creator's intent.
Translate every feature, service, or intellectual framework into its direct Financial, Emotional, Physical, or Spiritual consequence for the client.
Assign distinct, uncompetitive visual weights to different semantic roles in the content, ensuring the viewer's eye is routed exactly where it needs to go, in the exact order it needs to go there.
Treat every piece of content not as an end in itself, but as a touchpoint designed to advance a relationship, build trust, or invite further connection.
Design the sales sequence as a series of low-friction, escalating invitations based on demonstrated trust, rather than a single, high-pressure conversion attempt.
Remove shame, judgment, and exposure from the user's path, replacing them with interactions that preserve the user's dignity and autonomy.
Select and deploy typography that perfectly mirrors and amplifies the intended psychological archetype and emotional stance of the message.
Explicitly name who the offer or content is NOT for, using polarizing clarity to disqualify bad fits and compound trust with the ideal audience.
Anchor all visual assets, UI elements, and text blocks to an invisible structural grid to create predictable rhythm, alignment, and cognitive ease for the user.
Shape the user experience around meaningful points of entry, commitment, and completion rather than optimizing for pure, frictionless sameness.
Design the interface, visual asset, or text prompt so that the desired next action feels completely obvious, available, and behaviorally inviting.
Separate the creative generation phase from the critical validation phase, never allowing them to operate simultaneously within the same agent or prompt.
Identify the irreducible, unfunny truth that the content must communicate, then conceal it inside a comedic structure so the audience discovers it themselves rather than having it stated.

Select and assign a specific Funny Filter (or layered combination) to the generation pipeline as a rigid structural constraint, replacing the vague instruction 'be funny' with a precise distortion algorithm.

Take the inherent contrast or exaggeration in a concept and multiply it until it shatters the bounds of physics, logic, or social acceptability, creating a chasm so wide the audience must laugh to bridge it.

When adopting any voice, format, or persona in content, reproduce every detail — typography, cadence, syntax, visual style — with such precision that the audience cannot distinguish the reproduction from the original without examining the content payload.
Identify the core 'reveal' or anomalous word in a comedic sentence or visual frame, and restructure the grammar/layout to ensure it arrives at the absolute final millisecond of audience attention.
Generate 10-100x more material than needed, then ruthlessly prune to the top 5-10% using objective evaluation criteria.
Establish clear, simple character traits and then generate laughter by showing the character acting predictably according to those traits.
Cast the coach's voice or persona into stark opposition with the expected tone, audience assumptions, or industry norms to generate instant tension-based comedy.
Assign a culturally familiar archetype to any narrative figure — the coach, the client, or the antagonist — so the audience intuitively understands that figure's rules within seconds.
Compress any comic persona into a narrow representational function — 2-3 traits maximum — instead of building full three-dimensional character profiles.
Observe real humans — their strange traits, verbal tics, behavioral patterns — and translate those live observations into comedic material that carries the texture of actual lived experience.
Encode every piece of generated content with an implicit, overriding emotional broadcast: 'I am thrilled to be here talking to you about this.'

Treat the coach's persona as a testable hypothesis. Deploy content across multiple emotional registers and topics, measure audience response, then iteratively amplify the registers and topics that produce the deepest engagement — allowing the audience to carve the persona over time.

Strip the setup down to a single subject and a clear attitude, place the payload at the terminal word, and whenever possible, string multiple payloads off a single setup to create an uninterrupted cascade of releases.

Assign an explicit, high-intensity emotional label to every content block, then validate that the generated text's semantic tone matches the declared emotion — rejecting any output that reads as emotionally ambiguous or neutral.

Deploy content, measure audience response, then trace backward from engagement spikes to identify and isolate the specific structural and emotional variables that caused them — letting the audience's behavior sculpt future output.
Position the coach as someone actively wrestling with a real, current, genuine struggle — never as an invulnerable authority dispensing wisdom from a completed journey.

Inject calibrated, pattern-matched imperfections into generated output so that the content passes the audience's implicit 'is this a real human?' heuristic — manufacturing the illusion of live, spontaneous thought.
Express the polar opposite of the Subtext with total conviction, never breaking voice, and let the audience resolve the contradiction to discover the hidden truth.

Embody a clearly defined, 1-3 trait character (typically the Enemy or Past Self) to voice uncomfortable truths that the coach's authentic voice cannot safely deliver — creating a mirror for the audience's self-recognition.
Drop precise, mundane, hyper-specific details that only the target audience recognizes — brand names, exact phrasings, micro-behavioral patterns — to create the shock of being seen.
Explicitly acknowledge the persuasive, comedic, or structural machinery of the content itself, converting the audience from passive targets into co-conspirators who see behind the curtain.
Map the coach's complex or painful truth onto a structurally parallel but tonally absurd domain, revealing one half overtly while keeping the real-world half hidden for the audience to discover.

Focus all attention, enthusiasm, and analytical energy on something trivially related to the Subtext — while conspicuously ignoring the massive, obvious truth that the audience cannot help but notice.

Take the audience's specific limiting belief or catastrophic fear and mathematically scale it through 3-5 escalation steps until the final consequence is so physically impossible that the original fear is revealed as absurd.

Mine the coach's authentic struggles, past failures, industry frustrations, and personal anxieties — classifying each as Hard, Weird, Scary, or Stupid — and use these as the foundational raw material for all content generation.
Shift abruptly from describing a concept in past or abstract tense to embodying it in present tense — performing a mini-scene rather than narrating one.

Define the Topic, inject a polarizing Attitude (Hard, Weird, Scary, or Stupid), establish a non-obvious Premise that logically justifies the attitude, then deliver a Payoff that exploits the Premise's hidden logic.

Collide two fiercely unconnected domains with a single logical thread, producing a visual, absurd, and intellectually stimulating juxtaposition that makes the abstract concrete and the familiar strange.
List two congruent items that establish a clear pattern, then deliver a third item that violently subverts the established expectation — the Turn.

Reconstruct a real or anticipated interaction as a mini-dialogue where the performer delivers the perfect response they didn't (or wouldn't) say in real life — asserting retroactive mastery over a situation of powerlessness.

Submit every generated content draft to an adversarial Critic agent whose sole purpose is to punch up the humor — adding Mixes, expanding Act-Outs, strengthening Premises, and enforcing structural compliance.

Before writing any content, generate a radial association map from the core topic — listing clichés, related phrases, opposites, phonetic neighbors, and cultural touchpoints — then select the most unexpected associations as the raw material for metaphors and humor.
Before generating any content, assign a specific, heightened emotional stance toward the topic — never allow content to emerge from a neutral or balanced perspective.

Replace every generic noun, generic adjective, and generic scenario in generated content with a hyper-specific, visceral, culturally grounded detail — a proper noun, a precise number, a brand name, a sensory texture.
Clearly and definitively establish a rigid assumption in the audience's mind, then shatter it with a surprising alternative that is logically sound but emotionally unexpected.

Take a standard premise, practice, or belief system and ask "What if we followed this logic completely?" — extrapolating through 3-5 steps until the conclusion reveals the premise's inherent absurdity.
Identify the specific, uncomfortable truth that everyone in the audience implicitly knows but no one has explicitly articulated — then state it with precision and without apology.
Reduce scale drastically to a single, concrete moment or detail to approach truth without cognitive overwhelm.
Separate access from evaluation by demanding a completely unpolished, uninhibited first pass.
Observe a specific detail or moment patiently without forcing a thesis, allowing the true meaning to emerge organically.
Explicitly name and separate quiet intuitive truth from the loud, performative noise of the ego.
Ensure the piece stands for something beyond competence by explicitly grounding it in a lived ethical conviction.
Recover owned voice by actively removing borrowed permission, imitation, and self-censorship.
Frame communication as an offering to a specific individual in need rather than a display of competence.
Distill the analysis into a single, undeniable sentence driven by a high-stakes performance verb (Change, Continue, or Finish) that demands a specific future reality.
Attach specific characters, tangible physical scale, and a clear adversary to abstract information so the audience can emotionally process the stakes.
Use targeted speeches, stories, ceremonies, and symbols to resolve the tension of the current change stage and propel the audience into the next phase.
Break the journey into distinct "stepping stones" that alternate between showing an event, compressing time, and interpreting its significance, preventing the story from stalling in exposition.
Immediately articulate what is desired, what opposing force is preventing it, and the emotional or physical cost of failing to achieve it.
Give every discrete unit of content a specific dramatic purpose, entering late, building pressure, and ending on an emotional or informational polarity shift.
Define the singular overarching desire driving performance.
Substitute personal emotional trigger for authentic reaction.
Let real stimulus justify real response for caused delivery.
Organize performance around a meaningful contrastive movement.
Build a deep, emotionally specific unsaid history.
Induce genuine emotional pre-state before initiating communication.
Accept reality and build upon it immediately.
Maintain absolute external simplicity with maximum internal passion.
Repeat exactly what you observe to force instinctive reaction.
Align physical body language with the emotional thesis.
Compress the whole idea into a title-level center of gravity that makes a specific argument rather than describing a category.
Hold attention by alternating pressure and relief, forcing the audience to wait for the resolution of a stated problem.
Design the argument from the audience's perspective first, explicitly identifying and integrating the strongest intelligent objection into the content.
Structure attention into reset-sized blocks by inserting a sharp format shift, question, or new tension loop every 8-10 minutes.
Balance an argument across logic, credibility, and emotion simultaneously, never allowing one channel to completely dominate the others.
Diagnose and eliminate moments where the content serves the creator's need to perform rather than the audience's need for relevance.
Treat explanation as an act of care rather than a display of expertise, using a single empathetic reframe to unlock complex material.
Sequence communication to establish trust, shared worldview, and positive intent before delivering any evidence of competence or intellectual authority.
Begin the narrative at the specific moment the status quo is disrupted, forcing the protagonist into action to restore balance.

canonical_name: The Four Intents of Authentic Delivery
core_move: Rehearse the internal emotional intent behind the communication rather than choreographing the physical or vocal mechanics of the delivery.
Execute the four sequential dependencies of persuasion in order, critically ensuring that arguments are framed around the audience's values, not the speaker's.
Simultaneously feed all three channels of motivation—Direction, Empathy, and Meaning—adjusting the ratio based on the audience's experience level and the stakes of the situation.
Structure identity transitions by explicitly linking past experiences to present capabilities, rooting the cause of change in enduring character traits rather than external events.
Classify the information into one of four quadrants (Declarative-Conceptual, Declarative-Data, Exploratory-Conceptual, Exploratory-Data) before selecting the visual format.

canonical_name: The What Is / What Could Be Contrast Engine
core_move: Continuously oscillate between validating the audience's current reality and revealing a superior future state, creating an irresistible structural tension.
Strip the speaker of the protagonist role; explicitly position the audience as the hero who must cross the threshold, and the speaker as the mentor who provides the magical tool.
Condense the core message into a single, complete sentence containing a unique point of view, explicit stakes, and the word "you," refusing to proceed with production until this is achieved.
Design and insert a deliberate, highly concentrated emotional or sensory peak (the S.T.A.R. moment) that perfectly encapsulates and amplifies the Big Idea.

Engineer contrast across three simultaneous planes: alternate between current reality and future vision (Content), between analytical data and emotional narrative (Emotion), and between standard and unexpected delivery formats (Delivery).
Explicitly define the starting and ending states of the audience on two axes—Inward (belief) and Outward (behavior)—before drafting any content.
Prioritize un-fakeable human signals (warmth, real-time responsiveness, and lived experience) over synthetic polish, using AI to structure the message but never to replace the messenger's presence.
Audit and restructure content to ensure a dominant allocation of narrative and emotional material (65%), supported by data (25%) and framed by credibility (10%).

Engineer a specific, high-intensity moment (via props, shocking stats, stark visuals, or vulnerable narrative) designed to trigger a dopamine release that flags the surrounding information as vital for survival/memory.
Enforce a hard stop of 18 minutes for any unbroken informational delivery, utilizing "soft breaks" (stories, videos, or interactivity) to reset cognitive load in longer formats.

canonical_name: The Rule of Three Message Architecture
core_move: Structure all content using a strict Message Map: one overarching headline (Big Idea) supported by exactly three core pillars, rejecting any structure that attempts to deliver four or more main points.
Execute a pre-recording diagnostic to ensure the speaker is operating from deep, authentic passion for the topic, leveraging biological 'mood contagion' to infect the audience with that same energy.
Engineer content to trigger multiple sensory pathways simultaneously—combining auditory narrative with vivid imagery, physical props, or interactive kinesthetic exercises.
Identify the speaker's innate delivery style (animated, reflective, professorial) and execute relentless preparation until the techniques vanish, leaving only authentic presence.
Define a single, specific idea in 15 words or fewer before drafting any content, and ruthlessly eliminate any material that does not directly serve or connect to this exact throughline.
Design content starting from the audience's existing worldview (not the coach's expertise), incrementally building the new idea using metaphors and concepts the audience already understands.

Dedicate the first 30-60 seconds of any engagement strictly to establishing a human connection (using vulnerability, humor, or deep eye contact) before introducing any substantive content or credentials.

Translate complex concepts using a strict sequence: identify the gap in current understanding, provide a structural metaphor, build the new model incrementally, define negative boundaries, and validate with concrete examples.
Identify deliveries that feel memorized but mechanical, and intervene with a rehearsal protocol designed to internalize the script so deeply that the speaker's natural prosody and presence return.

canonical_name: The Five Talk Tools as a Modular Palette
core_move: Designate a primary and secondary 'Talk Tool' (Narration, Explanation, Persuasion, or Revelation) for each segment of content, mapping the choice directly to the goal of the throughline.

canonical_name: Curiosity Ignition as the Opening Architecture
core_move: Design the first 60 seconds to instantly create a curiosity gap (via a dramatic statement, a puzzling question, or a stunning visual) while explicitly banning apologies, credentials, or administrative context-setting.
Detect the active conversation layer (practical, emotional, or social) and match it before attempting to redirect or advise.
Prove comprehension by summarizing the speaker's point in your own words and explicitly asking for confirmation before responding.
Identify the client's active Ego State and intentionally anchor your response in the Adult state to break crossed transactions.
Identify what the client is fighting that they cannot control, and strategically cease resistance to restore nervous system regulation.
Prescribe purposeless, joyful play as a biological intervention to force the nervous system out of chronic threat states.
Ask questions that target values, beliefs, and emotional experiences rather than objective facts.
Establish felt similarity and shared vulnerability to reduce resistance before introducing a challenge.
Separate the human identity from the behavioral issue to preserve the alliance while delivering correction.
Audit the cumulative inputs filling the client's stress capacity, and selectively remove inputs to prevent neurobiological overflow.
Prepare for the interaction by mentally charting goals, potential obstacles, and planned responses before speaking.
Identify the client's dominant meta-script (e.g., "Always", "Almost") and force them to engage with empirical evidence that contradicts it.
Refuse to adopt the "Rescuer" or "Persecutor" roles when faced with a "Victim," stepping completely out of the transactional triangle.
Calibrate your linguistic identity (register, diction, cultural shorthand) to mirror the audience's native communication style, forcing them to recognize you as a peer before you speak.
Use a genuinely funny narrative setup to bypass the audience's cognitive resistance, delivering a challenging diagnostic truth in the space created by their laughter.
Position yourself as the bridge between disparate groups or domains, leveraging your outsider status to diagnose systemic blind spots that insiders cannot see.
Reframe the client's or audience's obstacle by demonstrating systems literacy: mapping the exact rules of the constraint and identifying the exploitable loopholes within it.

Present a familiar problem or conflict exclusively from the perspective of the person or system the audience blames, making the opponent's logic completely comprehensible without excusing their actions.
Tell a highly specific, personal story where the characters and plot mechanics directly mirror the structural dynamics of the overarching concept or system you are teaching.
Reframe a catastrophic failure or trauma by immediately identifying the specific tactical advantage, perspective, or immunity the audience gained by surviving it.
Identify the single most efficient path to the desired outcome and brutally delete all alternative methods, caveats, and options from the presentation to eliminate decision fatigue.

Bracket every piece of factual data or instruction with an emotional contextualization that deliberately builds confidence, reduces anxiety, or increases tension, ensuring the information lands in an actionable biological state.

Draft content without filtering (the 'spew'), intentionally generating 2-5x more volume than needed, then run a second, ruthless editorial pass (the 'whittle') to assassinate any tangent that distracts from the primary outcome.

Filter every instruction or pitch through a checklist of the audience's internal ecology (asking: Does this threaten their identity? Is the effort perceived as worth it? Does this invalidate their values?) and rewrite any point that triggers an ecological rejection.

Map specific emotional states to specific modules of a presentation (e.g., Fear/Loneliness in the hook, Anger/Enthusiasm in the paradigm shift, Safety in the offer) to systematically manipulate the audience's biological readiness to act.

Force all content creation to begin by explicitly defining the 'Audience + Feeling + Result' variables, and relentlessly shrink the scope of the promised result until it can realistically be mapped out in 45-60 minutes.
Isolate one tiny, easily executable component of your massive framework and give it away for free with the explicit promise that executing it *today* will yield a measurable, immediate win.
Establish a formal, mutually beneficial partnership with a trusted node who actively  hosts the experience, rather than relying on passive link-sharing.
Stage the introduction so the guest experiences measurable value (such as an interview,  premium output, or benchmark) before any ask or pitch occurs.
Convert operational byproducts (e.g., interview footage, benchmark data, coaching  transcripts) into primary acquisition and trust-transfer tools.
Leverage a partner's existing audience and established trust rather than attempting  to build distribution from zero.
Deliver premium proof outputs that make the recipient feel proud and elevate their  status, explicitly avoiding overt platform branding or promotional hooks.
Deliver insight before the prospect enters commodity-comparison mode, shaping their  understanding of the problem to uniquely match your capability.
Change the prospect's understanding of their problem through commercial teaching  before introducing any specific product solution.
Tailor the insight and the 'Commercial Reframe' to the specific metrics, anxieties,  and vocabulary of the stakeholder's role.
Allow the prospect to sit with the discomfort of their problem (the tension)  without immediately offering discounts, soothing platitudes, or premature solutions.
Structure visual elements within the frame to force a specific sequence of eye movement.
Design the production workflow to naturally output the desired aesthetic quality.
Subordinate all stylistic choices to the specific communication intent of the artifact.
Insert deliberate, absolute silence into the sonic flow to create pressure and activate the listener's imagination.
Artificially emphasize the primary subject or message while deliberately suppressing competing visual elements.
Layer multiple sonic elements to build richness, while ruthlessly EQing and mixing to protect the primary signal.
Identify the core signal within a chaotic environment and aggressively crop, filter, or restructure the surroundings to highlight that signal.
Align all stylistic elements to support the narrative thesis, rejecting choices that look 'nice' but contradict the message.
Deconstruct a complex creative output into a sequence of objective, checkable decisions.
Use purely sonic cues to trigger the listener's internal visual imagination, making them an active participant in the narrative.
Deploy specific sonic events to direct the user's focus exactly when and where it is needed.
Manipulate the subject's proximity to the edges of the frame to induce specific psychological responses (tension or stability).

Use frame edges, scale relationships, contrast, leading lines, and figure-ground separation to construct a precise visual pathway that forces the viewer's eye to land on the primary narrative subject before exploring secondary context.

canonical_name: Light and Color as Emotional Architecture
core_move: Assign a specific narrative and emotional intent to the light (e.g., harsh high-noon sunlight for interrogation/truth; soft blue-hour for ambiguity/transition) and color grading (e.g., desaturated monochrome for austerity; warm tones for intimacy) rather than defaulting to generic 'cinematic' aesthetics.

Before capturing or generating an image, define the specific Character, their specific Location (which provides context/tone), and the specific Event (the action or change occurring), refusing to proceed if any of the three are missing.

Stop optimizing for one standalone image. Instead, build a sequenced set consisting of an establishing shot (context), a medium action shot (relationship), a close-up (emotion), and a cutaway detail (texture), moving the viewer through a cinematic progression.

Intentionally withhold one piece of visual information (e.g., have the subject look intently at something outside the frame, obscure the outcome of an action, or capture the moment *before* the climax) to create a 'question' the viewer's brain is compelled to answer.

Never generate or capture an image from a generic, flat, eye-level observation point without a specific reason. Instead, explicitly define the vantage point (low/high), the focal length (compressed/wide), and layer the image with foreground obstructions to enforce a specific psychological relationship between the viewer and the subject.

During visual review or generation, aggressively protect and prioritize images that contain 'friction'—a messy desk, a genuinely exhausted look, an un-styled strand of hair, or a micro-expression— choosing the image that feels 'lived in' over the image that is technically flawless.

Never accept an image where focus is arbitrary or accidental. Explicitly assign meaning to the focal plane: use shallow focus to create intimacy/isolation by rejecting background context, use deep focus to force the subject to compete with their environment, and use rack focus to shift the narrative power dynamic in real-time.

Never select a focal length (or prompt an AI with a generic 'cinematic' style) without defining the emotional intent. Use a long telephoto to compress space (creating tension, claustrophobia, or hyper-focused intimacy). Use a wide-angle lens to expand space (creating vulnerability, vastness, or awkward exposure). Use a normal lens for objective, observational truth.

Before finalizing a visual, define the psychological relationship between the subject and the room/landscape. If the character feels trapped, compose the shot to make the walls literally close in on them. If the character feels lost, place them in a vast, empty space that visually overwhelms their scale. Force the environment to express the internal state of the subject.

Intentionally degrade or modify the technical perfection of a digital/AI image by introducing specific optical flaws (e.g., vintage lens softness, film grain, analog flare, or color bias). Use these intangibles not as filters, but as evidence of humanity, time, and documentary presence.

Stop optimizing individual images for maximum aesthetic impact. Instead, establish a baseline visual language (e.g., all early webinar slides use objective, wide, deep-focus shots). Then, at the precise moment of the narrative climax or paradigm shift, break the system (e.g., switch to a highly subjective, compressed telephoto shot). The contrast is the message.

Do not generate 'action' purely by having the subject run or move. Instead, manipulate the lens choice to change the *feel* of the motion. If the goal is frantic urgency or explosive action, use a wide-angle lens close to the subject to make lateral movement feel incredibly fast. If the goal is suspense or a 'nightmare' feeling of running but getting nowhere, use a long telephoto lens to compress the z-axis and visually slow down the approach.

Do not use heavy visual distortion simply because it looks 'cool' or 'edgy.' Only deploy severe optical warping when the narrative explicitly demands that the audience experiences a subjective, altered state of mind (e.g., panic, revelation, madness, or extreme dominance).

Before generating or mixing audio, define the 'Sovereign Signal' for the sequence. If the coach's voice is sovereign, aggressively duck (lower the volume of) the music and remove competing frequencies. If an emotional swell is sovereign, let the music overpower the environment. Never allow multiple layers to compete at equal volume; forced hierarchy is what creates narrative focus.

Do not default to standard, 'clean' broadcast audio for every asset. Explicitly define the 'Point of Audition.' If the narrative requires extreme intimacy or internal confession, use a hyper-dry, close-mic sound with zero room reflection. If the narrative requires alienation or disorientation, use extreme reverberation, low-pass filtering (muffling), or 'surveillance' tape aesthetics to distance the listener.

Stop 'Mickey-Mousing' the emotion (matching every visual action with a perfectly correlated sound). Instead, identify the hidden truth of the scene and score *that*. If the image shows a character achieving massive financial success but feeling hollow, do not play triumphant music. Play sparse, cold, isolating room tone. Use asynchronous sound, overlapping dialogue, or genre-clashing music to create a psychological gap between what is seen and what is felt.

Assign a 'Density Budget' to every sequence. When the coach is delivering complex instructional data, the budget should be 1 (Voice only, or Voice + 1 minimal pad). When the narrative reaches a chaotic inflection point, the budget can expand to 5 (Voice + Drums + Bass + Melody + SFX). Immediately after the climax, crash the density back to 0 (Silence) to let the emotional payload reverberate in the listener's mind.

Establish a unique Sonic Motif for a specific coach, archetype, or conversion phase. Introduce it subtly during the 'Hook.' When the coach delivers their core philosophical truth (the 'Aha!' moment), bring the motif back to the foreground. By linking the sonic signature to the psychological payoff, you train the listener's nervous system to recognize and trust the creator across multiple platforms and formats.

Stop trying to fix bad audio by rewriting the prompt. Fix it by upgrading the pipeline. If a track is muddy, do not prompt for 'clearer mix.' Instead, force the workflow to output isolated stems (Voice, Bass, Drums, Melody), and build a deterministic ducking algorithm to algorithmically carve out frequencies. Treat aesthetic problems as engineering problems.

canonical_name: Playback Context Is Part of the Composition
core_move: Optimize all audio for the lowest common denominator of playback. Center-weight the vocals (do not rely on wide stereo panning for the main message). Cut unnecessary low-end frequencies (which phone speakers cannot reproduce anyway) to clear up the midrange. Ensure mono compatibility so the track survives being played through a single speaker.
Write so the message remains clear and memorable even when heard once in imperfect attention conditions.
Speak directly to a single imagined listener to create psychological closeness.

canonical_name: Writing for the Ear, Not the Eye
core_move: Draft content specifically for spoken rhythm, breathing cadence, and auditory processing.
Test every spoken line aloud before shipping it so dead phrasing and synthetic rhythm are caught early.
Validate that audio feels relatable, engaging, authentic, and liberating before adding more polish.
Maximize the first and last lines because they disproportionately shape emotion, memory, and continuation.
Use sound and speech to make the listener visualize the scene instead of merely receiving information.
Make the opening orientation and closing handoff structurally deliberate rather than improvised.
Use vivid sensory cues to build the theater of the mind for the listener.
Compress aggressively until only the most signal-rich spoken material remains.
Use proximity, mic feel, and acoustic nearness to increase disclosure, trust, and warmth.
Create better spoken output by listening actively and intervening minimally so the subject's real signal can surface.

canonical_name: First Major Win-State Before Social Expansion
core_move: Delay all social expansion requests until the user has achieved their first emotionally satisfying win within the product.

Architect the feedback loops so that the moment the user completes an action, the system immediately returns a score or visual response that explicitly explains *why* the action mattered to the user's specific progression.

Audit the entire platform for generic gamification terms ('Points', 'Levels', 'Coins', 'Badges') and replace them with precise, executive-coaching vocabulary that reflects the user's actual psychological development.

Isolate the absolute peak moment of the user's journey (e.g., receiving their first CPSC evaluation) and engineer a 2-second visual/haptic animation sequence that builds anticipation and releases it in a satisfying, high-fidelity crescendo.

Instead of only showing session-specific scores, design the dashboard and push notifications to aggregate and celebrate hidden lifetime metrics, transforming small daily actions into massive, undeniable proof of effort over time.

Format all scorecards and dashboards so that what they did right (and the support they received) occupies 80% of the screen, hiding the critical/negative feedback behind a 'View Details' or 'Areas for Improvement' accordion.

Append a highly visible 'Redemption Round' or 'Try Again' button directly to the bottom of every single AI scorecard and feedback message, ensuring the user never hits a dead end after being told how to improve.

Eliminate visual noise, delete all instructional "happy talk," and highlight the single most important next action with a massive, unambiguous visual cue (the Glowing Choice) to ensure zero-thought progression.

Design the entry point of every interaction as an emotional, intuitive reaction (e.g., swiping, reacting to a visual), delaying any analytical tasks (e.g., formulating a complex thesis) until the user has already made micro-commitments.
Systematically identify and remove any requirement that demands excessive time, physical taps, mental analysis, or social risk from the core 'Record a Reaction' loop.
Architect the software and the content delivery so that the user's phone is always primed to record, bypassing all traditional app-loading, login, and context-gathering friction.
Transform mundane functional taps (e.g., 'Submit') into deliberate, physical 'Commitment Swipes' or 'Hold-to-Lock' gestures that generate agency rather than passivity.
Identify elements of the UI that typically cause stress (timers, scores, public juries) and use copywriting and visual cues to reframe them as playful, low-stakes, exploratory tools.

Use an agent persona that mirrors the user's explicit intent and dynamically adapts its recommendations and tone based on historical interactions, actively protecting their status and minimizing their cognitive friction.

During high-anxiety onboarding or before the first recording, explicitly prompt the user to choose an 'Expert Persona' or 'Heroic Archetype'. Address them by this identity throughout the experience to decouple their performance from their personal insecurities.

Immediately after delivering a variable reward (like a high Delivery Score), prompt the user to make a small, permanent investment in the platform (e.g., 'Save this vocal take to your Master Archive' or 'Pin this stance to your public profile').

Do not present a massive menu of options (e.g., 'Choose from 50 debate topics'). Instead, use the user's historical data and archetype to present ONE highly tailored suggestion (e.g., 'This topic matches your Voice DNA. Record now.').

Whenever the user's performance metrics are low, aggressively scrub the UI of any static nouns or red-colored failure states. Replace them with active verbs that describe the skill they are currently 'building' or 'forging', reinforcing a growth identity.

Never award badges, points, or status for meaningless administrative tasks. Only attach gamified rewards and identity markers to actions that demonstrably increase the coach's actual real-world speaking competency or business authority.
Eliminate all latency between the Action and the Variable Reward, and ensure the Reward screen immediately prompts the next Investment to chain loops together at maximum velocity.
Architect the platform as a state-changing progression system where the available features, required skill level, and visible metrics unlock sequentially based on the user's maturity phase.
Design rare, high-stakes culmination events that require the synthesis of all previously practiced skills, resulting in a disproportionately large status or visibility reward.
Design analytics and progress visualization to explicitly highlight cumulative gains over weeks or months, demonstrating that even a 'failed' daily drill contributes to a successful long-term trend.

Replace functional loading screens and basic onboarding instructions with highly theatrical, premium 'calibration' sequences that instill the belief that the system is actively enhancing the user's environment and potential.

Rewrite every major task prompt to explicitly include a 'Belief Injector'—a sentence or data point that proves to the user that success is not just possible, but highly probable for someone with their specific identity.
Continuously rotate topics, introduce temporary seasonal formats, and embed the core mechanics into current, real-world events so the user never feels they are repeating the exact same task twice.

canonical_name: White Hat -> Black Hat -> White Hat Emotional Sequencing
core_move: Never end a user loop on a Black Hat state (fear, urgency, loss). Always force a transition into a White Hat state (meaning, accomplishment, ownership) immediately following a failure or stressful event.
Gate retries behind a finite constraint (time, quantity, or specific condition) while explicitly adjusting the difficulty or providing a hint to ensure the user believes the win is now within reach.
Use explicit UI copy to reframe necessary friction, rules, and constraints as psychological tools designed to help the user, completely avoiding punitive or bureaucratic language.

Guarantee that the user controls the visibility of their failures, ensuring that bad takes are never automatically published, and that the cost of failing a drill is strictly contained within the game loop.
When a user returns from a period of inactivity or failure, immediately offer a frictionless, zero-guilt action to reintegrate them, explicitly bypassing penalties or 'catch-up' mechanics.
Program the system to detect behavioral signatures of frustration or giving up, and instantly dispatch an async intervention that validates their partial effort and removes the immediate pressure.
Design high-status rewards that strictly require the participation, votes, or gifts of other human users, explicitly blocking solo attainment.
Design shareable artifacts that act as verifiable signals of high status, competence, or authority, making the act of sharing an ego-driven reward.
Filter and surface social proof, leaderboard data, and community activity exclusively based on identity proximity (niche, tier, struggle) rather than global popularity.

Design the status ecosystem with multiple independent lanes, periodic resets, and tiered brackets so every user experiences upward mobility while protecting the legacy achievements of long-term users.
Create multiple, distinct participation roles within the ecosystem that require lower friction to enter but still directly impact the outcomes of the high-friction users.
Systematically pair external notifications with moments where the user is likely experiencing a specific internal emotional pain point, until the external prompt is no longer needed.
Force rapid, near-daily engagement by compressing the time between action and variable reward, ensuring the user completes the entire loop before leaving.
Monitor telemetry for spikes in user motivation or ability, and trigger requests or escalations exclusively within those narrow opportune windows.
Use real-time biometric and semantic telemetry to tailor all feedback and prompts, completely eliminating generic 'best practice' notifications.
Gate all social and expansion triggers behind a mathematically proven, hard-won success state, completely suppressing them during onboarding or failure.
Anchor all system prompts to real-world context, external events, or the user's immediate environment, completely avoiding self-referential 'app-centric' notifications.
Map routine prompts explicitly to the user's biological and professional calendar rhythms, ensuring the system integrates into existing habits rather than demanding new ones.
Frame every system trigger, score, and notification explicitly around the user's intrinsic goal of professional mastery, never around the extrinsic goal of collecting points within the app.
Sequence challenge triggers from minimum-viable-effort to high-complexity, gating advanced triggers until the foundational behaviors have become automatic.
Trigger an immediate, positive emotional state by front-loading visually stunning, high-contrast aesthetics before the user encounters any logical or behavioral task.
Frame performance data and system feedback using elite, professional language and high-end visual design that appeals directly to the user's highest self-image.

Design the output artifact (video, image, or link preview) to function primarily as a high-status credential for the sender, completely bypassing the social friction of traditional 'refer-a-friend' mechanics.
Frame the user's micro-actions (recording, voting, debating) as vital contributions to a high-stakes, industry-wide mission that cannot succeed without their specific expertise.
Instantly detect performance failures and intercept the resulting user shame by framing the failure gracefully and offering an immediate, highly supported path to retry and achieve 'Fiero'.
